# Jacob C. Smith

> Independent systems engineer, AI red-teamer, and consciousness researcher
> based in Buckhannon, West Virginia. Tagline: "I help teams turn messy reality
> into measurable systems." Currently shipping the WVRTP facility inspection
> system for Readyfuels (https://readyfuels.com) and the Hermes plugin for
> Nous Research (https://nousresearch.com). Practice areas: operational
> analytics, AI red-teaming, local-first AI systems, and decision-architecture
> audits.

## About
- Site: https://jacobcdsmith.github.io
- Email: jacobcsmithd@gmail.com
- Phone: (304) 473-9980
- GitHub: https://github.com/Jacobcdsmith
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/jacobcsmith
- Location: Buckhannon, West Virginia, USA
- Availability: Open for new engagements (remote, worldwide)

## Services
- Operational analytics engagements (SQL, Python, BI dashboards, forecasts,
  formula-heavy KPI / safety scoring systems — proven on the WVRTP work for
  Readyfuels)
- AI red-teaming and safety reviews for LLM-powered features and agent stacks
  (delivery example: red-team subagent inside the Hermes plugin for
  Nous Research)
- Local-first AI systems (JCLAW, MCPStarfleetCommand, a local WebSocket MCP
  gateway; llama.cpp / Ollama / vLLM where appropriate)
- Systems audits and decision architecture for founders and ops leaders who
  need an outside systems-thinker to map what is actually happening and what
  to do next

## Active Projects
- EMERGENT-MCF-EI: GPU-accelerated lattice simulation modeling consciousness
  as a spectral filter; forthcoming preprint targeting arXiv q-bio.NC.
- JCLAW: local-first LLM runtime with multi-provider routing, persistent
  sessions, conversation branching, agentic loops, and MCP dual-mode
  operation.
- Hermes plugin (for Nous Research): three-subagent stack (codegen,
  red-team, resource-gathering) under a persistent SOUL.md identity layer
  with OpenRouter routing.
- WVRTP Facility Inspection System (for Readyfuels): React/Vite/TypeScript
  app on Vercel with QR-driven capture, Power Automate pipelines, and a
  companion 1,135-formula safety + KPI Excel workbook.
